core_mechanism: mean-field / propagation-of-chaos limit: reduction of a high-dimensional linear many-body evolution to a self-consistent nonlinear single-unit (mean-field) equation as the number of interacting components N grows large, with quantitative bounds on the rate at which the exact system's one-body marginal converges to the mean-field solution
brief_reason: The abstract derives an effective nonlinear one-body (Hartree) dynamics as the large-N limit of the microscopic N-body (Schroedinger) evolution and proves rate-of-convergence bounds on the difference between the one-particle marginal density and the mean-field solution.
